As you follow the path of damage slightly northeast from that collapsed shed, it appears to have gone through a field at 1832 Mt Tabor, but stayed between the barn and garden at that address. The National Weather Service in cooperation with the Watauga County NC Emergency Manager determined that a tornado had touched down near the intersection of Birch Drive and Hill Street in Blowing Rock NC before traveling northeast for approximately 0.6 mil and then lifting northeast of Chetola Lake Drive. Several softwood and a few hardwood trees were uprooted and snapped along the path of the tornado. In addition, a few free standing tents near Bass Lake and Chetola Lake Drive were also blown down. This tornado occurred with the outer peripheral circulation of Hurricane Helene in what is known as a Predecessor Rain Event or PRE.

*** 1 INJ *** A Storm Survey Conducted by the National Weather Service in Blacksburg VA confirmed that an EF2 tornado associated with the remnants of former Hurricane Helene initially touched down about three miles west-southwest of Dry Fork and traveled just under seven miles to the north-northwest to a point approximately six miles west of Chatham. Along the path of the tornado, around 30 structures were damaged, including one mobile home that was completely destroyed. Numerous hardwood and softwood trees were snapped and/or uprooted. The tornado remained on the ground for around 18 minutes. One female was injured during the tornado while residing in her mobile home.
